summary: Matched the early RObj animation helpers through HSD_RObjAddAnimAll, moved several RObj routines out of assembly into C, labeled remaining RObj symbols, and cleaned up forced inline helper duplication across sysdolphin now that `-inline auto` handled those cases. The PR intentionally stopped before HSD_RObjGetGlobalPosition because matching it would require inlining a JObj function.
what_changed: Implemented RObj animation-list helpers in C: HSD_RObjRemoveAnimByFlags, HSD_RObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags, HSD_RObjRemoveAnimAll, HSD_RObjReqAnimByFlags, HSD_RObjReqAnimAllByFlags, HSD_RObjReqAnimAll, HSD_RObjAddAnim, and HSD_RObjAddAnimAll.
Changed HSD_RObjAnim from `inline` to a normal function and kept HSD_RObjAnimAll as the public list iterator.
Removed a large assembly block from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.s corresponding to matched RObj routines up to HSD_RObjAddAnimAll.
Added RObj prototypes to src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.h for the newly matched/all-list helpers.
Renamed many remaining RObj and Rvalue assembly symbols from anonymous func_ labels to descriptive HSD names in asm and include/functions.h.

Updated call sites in JObj, WObj, and melee lb assembly to call named RObj symbols such as HSD_RObjAlloc, HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj, HSD_RObjRemove, HSD_RObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags, HSD_RObjReqAnimAllByFlags, HSD_RObjAddAnimAll, HSD_RObjLoadDesc, HSD_RObjResolveRefsAll, HSD_RObjUpdateAll, HSD_RObjGetGlobalPosition, and HSD_RObjRemoveAll.
Cleaned up forced inline wrappers across sysdolphin by making helpers normal functions or calling the existing non-inline version directly in DObj, TObj, FObj, TExp, CObj, and PObj.
smart_moves: Used the compiler's `-inline auto` behavior instead of maintaining duplicate forced-inline helper bodies, reducing source noise while preserving matching where possible.
Matched the RObj helpers as small composable single-object and all-list functions: the All variants iterate linked lists and delegate to the per-object helpers.
Kept flag-filtered behavior explicit: RObj animation operations check `(flags & 0x80) != 0` before removing or requesting AObj animation.
Labeled not-yet-decompiled assembly symbols with their likely HSD names, which immediately improved call-site readability across JObj, WObj, and melee lb assembly.
Stopped before HSD_RObjGetGlobalPosition because it required a JObj inline dependency; this avoided pulling unrelated JObj decomp work into the PR.
decomp_lessons: When `-inline auto` already reproduces desired codegen, prefer deleting forced inline duplicates and calling the canonical function to reduce maintenance burden.
For baselib linked-list helpers, a reliable pattern is: null guard, iterate via `next`, and call a per-node helper; this matched RObj RemoveAnim/ReqAnim/AddAnim All variants.
Matching one subsystem can unlock meaningful symbol propagation in neighboring assembly files even before those neighbors are decompiled.
Do not force a match past a subsystem boundary if it requires unready inline behavior from another module; document the blocker and stop at a clean boundary.
Header updates should accompany newly matched functions so later files can call named APIs rather than anonymous labels.
naming_conventions: RObj public API names use the HSD_RObj prefix, e.g. HSD_RObjRemoveAnimAllByFlags, HSD_RObjReqAnimAllByFlags, HSD_RObjAddAnimAll.
`All` suffix indicates iteration across a linked list of objects.
`ByFlags` suffix indicates the operation is gated by an animation flag mask.
Rvalue-related functions use HSD_Rvalue prefix, e.g. HSD_RvalueRemoveAll and HSD_RvalueResolveRefsAll.
Internal resolver helpers in include/functions.h were labeled descriptively where possible, e.g. set_dirup_matrix, resolveCnsDirUp, resolveCnsOrientation, resolveLimits.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted matched RObj assembly functions into C and removed their assembly bodies from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.s.
Renamed assembly globals and branch targets without changing instruction bytes where functions remained in assembly.
Replaced anonymous call labels in assembly with named symbols to preserve binary behavior while improving semantic traceability.
Used ordinary C null guards and linked-list loops to match RObj animation helper code.
Removed inline-only duplicate helpers such as HSD_DObjModifyFlags_inline, HSD_TObjReqAnimAllByFlags_inline, and HSD_FObjStopAnim_inline in favor of canonical callable functions.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit rationale in the PR text is the author's note that HSD_RObjGetGlobalPosition was deferred because it requires a JObj function to be inlined.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that matches HSD_RObjGetGlobalPosition and the required JObj inline dependency.
Search for subsequent cleanup of remaining RObj assembly labels such as set_dirup_matrix, resolveCnsDirUp, resolveCnsOrientation, and resolveLimits.
Check whether the forced inline cleanup pattern from this PR was reused in other sysdolphin baselib modules.
